column1: # Based on UN romanization of Persian (UNGEGN) and common conventions
# Persian shares the Arabic script but has 4 extra letters (پ چ ژ گ),
# different vowel pronunciation, and distinct romanization conventions.
# The default table already provides base Arabic mappings; this table
# overrides characters where Persian pronunciation differs from Arabic.
# === Persian-specific letters (already in default, but explicit) ===
# Arabic ع (ain) → Persian typically omits or uses "'"
# Arabic ك (kaf, U+0643) → Persian uses U+06A9 (kaf) instead,
# but in case Arabic kaf appears: "k"
# Waw (و) — can be "v", "u", "o" in Persian; default to "v" for consonantal
# Arabic yaa (ي, U+064A) → in Persian context: "y"
# shadda — gemination (doubled consonant, handled by push)
# Heh goal (ہ) — sometimes used in Persian
